title: Pleuropteropyrum weyrichii
taxonomy: Pleuropteropyrum weyrichii is a flowering plant species that belongs to the family Polygonaceae. This family is commonly known as the buckwheat or smartweed family, which consists of various types of plants with similar characteristics. The genus Pleuropteropyrum is a monotypic genus, indicating that it contains only one recognized species, which is P. weyrichii. This plant is native to Asia and is often found in mountainous regions. As a member of the Polygonaceae family, Pleuropteropyrum weyrichii is closely related to other plants with similar morphological features.

intro: Pleuropteropyrum weyrichii, also known as the Korean mountain ash, is a species of flowering plant in the family Rosaceae. This plant is valued for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antimicrobial effects, making it useful in treating various health conditions such as fever, rheumatism, and digestive issues. The bioactive constituents of Pleuropteropyrum weyrichii include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and terpenoids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. The most common herbal preparations made with this plant are teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are often used in traditional Korean medicine to promote health and well-being.
